checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet - jump coincides with FY2012 revenue growth of ~12% (16112181224.1 vs 14392719313.41), consistent with an acquisition rather than organic capitalization

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: Across all annual periods 2009-2017 operating cash flow consistently exceeds net income (e.g., NetIncomeLoss=1739387047.31 vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1480940615.1 for FY2011 is the lone exception)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.location: 10-Q balance sheet, up 4.9% while SalesRevenueGoodsNet fell 5.7% FY2017 vs FY2016

evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsSold=2378539002.41 on SalesRevenueGoodsNet=3645719656.73 (2017-05-29 to 2017-08-27), implied margin ~34.75%, a ~150bps YoY decline

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K quarterly data - shows a sharp sequential earnings drop, inconsistent with artificially smoothed earnings

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly figures range from 180680365.03 to 593788415.92 across the sample, indicating meaningful volatility rather than unnatural smoothness

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.location: 10-K note disclosure - allowance fell ~18% even as ReceivablesNetCurrent grew from 1316219704.15 (2016-05-29) to 1383249411.31 (2017-05-28), reducing the reserve coverage ratio from ~2.18% to ~1.70% of receivables

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K/A filing_date=2003-07-30 and 10-Q/A filing_date=2003-07-30, preceded by NT 11-K filing_date=2003-11-26

evidence.location: filing_chronology - pattern of periodic restatement/amendment activity historically, though none occur within the most recent 2009-2017 window covered by the detailed financial series

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data points for Assets, Liabilities, Revenue, Receivables, Inventory, Cash Flow and Allowance accounts are available continuously from fiscal 2007/2009 through fiscal 2017 (e.g., SalesRevenueGoodsNet series spans 2007-05-28 through 2017-08-27)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduced bad-debt/allowance reserve relative to a growing receivables balance in a period of declining revenue, which understates bad debt expense and overstates net receivables and net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=28630293.39 (2016-05-29) declining to 23503923.29 (2017-05-28) while ReceivablesNetCurrent grew from 1316219704.15 to 1383249411.31 over the same period

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential aggressive revenue recognition or extended credit terms (channel stuffing) to prop up sales as top-line growth turned negative, evidenced by receivables rising even as reported revenue fell.

rationale_evidence.quote: SalesRevenueGoodsNet fell from 16020486906.09 (FY2016) to 15108089752.26 (FY2017) while ReceivablesNetCurrent rose from 1316219704.15 to 1383249411.31 over the same fiscal year-end dates

Quarterly comparison shows SalesRevenueGoodsNet declining from 3779875795.01 (Q1 FY2017, filed prior year) to 3645719656.73 (Q1 FY2018) while ReceivablesNetCurrent rose from 1398725245.57 (2016-08-28) to 1495836105.58 (2017-08-27)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Inventory build-up amid declining revenue and compressing gross margin suggests possible insufficient markdown/obsolescence reserves, which would understate cost of goods sold and overstate inventory/gross profit.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet rose from 1367386681.19 (2016-05-29) to 1434996732.13 (2017-05-28) even as SalesRevenueGoodsNet declined from 16020486906.09 to 15108089752.26 over the same period

Gross margin declined from ~36.3% (GrossProfit=1370481848.04 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=3779875795.01, quarter ended 2016-08-28) to ~34.75% (implied from CostOfGoodsSold=2378539002.41 / SalesRevenueGoodsNet=3645719656.73, quarter ended 2017-08-27)

top_signals: Receivables grew (+5.1% FY2017, +6.9% latest quarter YoY) while revenue declined (-5.7% FY2017, -3.6% latest quarter YoY), a classic divergence flag
Allowance for doubtful accounts as % of receivables fell from ~2.18% (FY2016) to ~1.70% (FY2017) despite receivables growth, reducing reserve coverage in a weakening revenue environment
Inventory grew 4.9% in FY2017 while revenue fell 5.7%, accompanied by a ~150bps YoY gross margin compression in the most recent comparable quarter
Historical pattern of 10-K/A and 10-Q/A amendments (2007, 2003, 2000, 1996) and an NT 11-K filing, though all predate the core 2009-2017 analysis window
Operating cash flow consistently exceeds net income across nearly all periods, which is a mitigating (not aggravating) factor against earnings manipulation concerns
